name: Knowledge Brain
tagline: Queryable institutional memory with a citation on every answer — or no answer
plain_summary: Knowledge Brain turns fictional meeting transcripts into a deterministic TF-IDF index for cited questions, paste-ready footnotes, meeting prep, and review-remediation handoffs. Its remediation path generates a cited, apply-ready prompt and a PENDING change log; a downstream AI or operator owns execution and confirmation of the workpaper changes.
marketing_thesis: A knowledge system earns trust by carrying each quote back to its meeting, date, timestamp, and speaker — and by refusing to answer when no source clears the relevance floor.
layers_intro: The stack preserves chain of custody from the transcript of record through retrieval and the downstream handoff. Select a layer for its plain-terms and engineering detail.

layers.label: Downstream Handoff
layers.plain: Packages a reviewer's sourced instructions for the person or system that will perform the work.
layers.engineering: Remediation returns ordered ChangeDirectives, verbatim source quotes, a generated apply-ready prompt, and a one-to-one fix packet whose entries start PENDING. Execution and confirmation are downstream responsibilities.

layers.label: Cited Renderers
layers.plain: Formats answers, footnotes, meeting briefs, prompts, and change logs with their sources attached.
layers.engineering: The reporting layer composes every returned quote with a provenance tag containing title, date, HH:MM:SS timestamp, and speaker; empty retrieval renders the refusal banner.

layers.label: Deterministic Retrieval
layers.plain: Finds the most relevant recorded decisions without calling a model or a network service.
layers.engineering: A stdlib TF-IDF index scores knowledge cards by keyword and weighted topic tags. Results sort by descending score with card_id as the stable tie-break, and cards below MIN_RELEVANCE are excluded.

layers.label: Verbatim Extraction
layers.plain: Lifts selected statements and review requests from the transcript without rewriting them.
layers.engineering: ExtractionSpec and DirectiveSpec resolve timestamped utterances into KnowledgeCards and ChangeDirectives; rule_text and request_text are copied byte-for-byte from the matched utterance.

layers.label: Transcript of Record
layers.plain: Keeps the exact words, speaker, meeting, date, and time behind every retrieved item.
layers.engineering: Meeting and Utterance are the source-of-truth dataclasses. Provenance carries meeting_id, title, date, speaker, and HH:MM:SS timestamp with each card or directive.

blocks.label: Fictional Transcripts
blocks.engineering: Meeting + ordered Utterance tuples form the transcript of record

blocks.label: Verbatim Extractor
blocks.plain: selects recorded statements and review requests
blocks.engineering: timestamp lookup creates byte-identical KnowledgeCards and ChangeDirectives

blocks.label: Corpus + TF-IDF Index
blocks.plain: stores cited cards and ranks them by topic
blocks.engineering: BrainEngine builds a deterministic keyword/tag TF-IDF index over the fixed corpus

blocks.label: Ask · Cite · Prep
blocks.plain: answers a question, supplies one footnote, or prepares a meeting
blocks.engineering: ask returns ranked hits, cite filters to authoritative kinds, and prep separates settled positions from open items

blocks.label: Cited Output or Refusal
blocks.plain: returns sourced text or says there is no sourced answer
blocks.engineering: citation_block binds the verbatim card text to its provenance; empty hits render REFUSAL

blocks.label: Review Directives
blocks.plain: retrieves the reviewer's recorded corrections in spoken order
blocks.engineering: directives_for matches a review title or topic, then orders citations by meeting, timestamp, and directive_id

blocks.label: Cited Apply-Ready Prompt
blocks.plain: generates the prompt and a PENDING source-to-status map
blocks.engineering: Remediation contains ordered directives, the generated prompt, and one FixPacketEntry per directive at STATUS_PENDING

blocks.label: Downstream AI / Operator
blocks.plain: performs the requested work outside the Brain
blocks.engineering: external execution and confirmation boundary; the Brain's handoff ends with the cited prompt and PENDING log

spec_strip.label: Tests
spec_strip.source: py -3 -m pytest --collect-only -q -o addopts= (knowledge-brain-engine/); live collection verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
spec_strip.plain: the checks that pin this engine's behavior

spec_strip.label: Meetings ingested
spec_strip.source: python -m brain_engine (knowledge-brain-engine/) — live index reports 5 meetings; brain_engine/generate.py builds the five-meeting fictional corpus
spec_strip.plain: fictional transcripts in the runnable corpus

spec_strip.label: Knowledge cards
spec_strip.source: python -m brain_engine (knowledge-brain-engine/) — live index reports 15 knowledge cards
spec_strip.plain: cited decisions, rules, definitions, and open items

spec_strip.label: Card kinds
spec_strip.source: brain_engine/model.py CARD_KINDS — decision, rule, definition, open-item; confirmed by the live index
spec_strip.plain: three authoritative kinds plus parked open items

spec_strip.label: Review directives
spec_strip.source: python -m brain_engine remediate "Surplus Workpaper Review — Reviewer Corrections" — live CLI emits 4 cited directives in spoken order
spec_strip.plain: each mapped one-to-one to a PENDING log row

spec_strip.label: Refusal exit
spec_strip.source: brain_engine/cli.py main — ask, cite, prep, and remediate return 3 when no sourced result exists; confirmed with an off-topic live CLI query

problem_statement: Finance and tax decisions often survive only as meeting memory or buried notes. When the same position is needed in a workpaper, disclosure, or review response, an uncited paraphrase is not enough: the user needs the recorded words, who said them, and when — or an explicit refusal when the source is absent.

scenarios.title: A workpaper assertion gets one traceable footnote
scenarios.narrative: Cite mode filters out open items, returns the best authoritative card, and renders its transcript text byte-identically with the meeting title, date, timestamp, and speaker attached.
scenarios.source: python -m brain_engine --cite "return of capital in excess of basis is a deemed gain"; brain_engine/engine.py cite; brain_engine/report.py render_citation

scenarios.title: An off-topic question produces a machine-readable refusal
scenarios.narrative: When no card clears the relevance floor, the CLI prints “No sourced answer — the brain does not guess” and exits 3 instead of filling the gap with a plausible answer.
scenarios.source: python -m brain_engine ask "what is the office parking and lunch policy"; live exit 3; brain_engine/cli.py and brain_engine/report.py

scenarios.title: A review meeting becomes a cited prompt, not an untracked edit
scenarios.narrative: The remediation command retrieves four fictional reviewer directives in spoken order and generates an apply-ready prompt plus a one-to-one change log at PENDING. A downstream AI or operator performs the edits and confirms their status.
scenarios.source: python -m brain_engine remediate "Surplus Workpaper Review — Reviewer Corrections"; brain_engine/engine.py remediate; knowledge-brain-engine/README.md Review → remediation

scenarios.title: Stored knowledge drift is re-derived only where the transcript supports it
scenarios.narrative: The integrity demo starts with four injected findings, re-derives three text-or-speaker corrections across two turns, and leaves the orphaned citation quarantined. The final verdict is ESCALATED with exit 3 because no utterance exists to support that record.
scenarios.source: python -m brain_engine.loop --demo (knowledge-brain-engine/); live demo output; brain_engine/loop.py

instruction_set.output: meeting, card-kind, topic, and provenance catalogue

instruction_set.output: ranked cited answer(s) or explicit refusal
ranked cited answer(s) or explicit refusal
verbatim quote + provenance tag or refusal

instruction_set.operation: assemble cited prior positions and open items
generate a cited apply-ready remediation handoff

instruction_set.output: the unchanged hand-authored corpus; the current build ignores N
cited hits clearing R or refusal
index, citation, prep, remediation, and change-log files

instruction_set.operation: compare the clean knowledge corpus with its transcripts
inject the documented internal knowledge-drift profile
instruction_set.output: ESCALATED after supported drift is re-derived and the orphan is held

instruction_set.operation: accept the loop's compatibility seed argument
instruction_set.output: the same fixed-corpus integrity verdict; the current build ignores N
final CLEAN, REMEDIATED, or ESCALATED verdict

benchmarks.label: Engine tests
benchmarks.source: py -3 -m pytest --collect-only -q -o addopts= (knowledge-brain-engine/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json
benchmarks.plain: live collection, not the README's historical sample output

benchmarks.label: Indexed fictional corpus
benchmarks.source: python -m brain_engine — live index summary; brain_engine/generate.py build_corpus

benchmarks.label: Review handoff
benchmarks.unit: cited directives mapped one-to-one to PENDING entries
benchmarks.source: python -m brain_engine remediate "Surplus Workpaper Review — Reviewer Corrections" — live CLI output; brain_engine/engine.py

benchmarks.label: Integrity demo recovery
benchmarks.source: python -m brain_engine.loop --demo — live output reports 3 corrections, 2 turns, and 1 quarantined orphan

control_characteristics.plain: Every returned answer is tied to a recorded source. The remediation boundary ends with the generated cited prompt and PENDING log; execution and confirmation belong to the downstream user or system.

verdict_map.severity: All internal drift has transcript support
verdict_map.action: stored text or speaker is re-derived verbatim; exit 0

verdict_map.severity: Orphaned source or turn budget exhausted
verdict_map.action: unsupported entries are held for a human; exit 3

guarantees: Citation chain: returned card text is byte-identical to its transcript utterance, with meeting title, date, HH:MM:SS timestamp, and speaker attached.
Refuse-do-not-guess: ask, cite, prep, and remediation return no sourced content when retrieval or directive matching is empty.
Stable retrieval: fixed corpus and relevance floor produce the same scores and ranked order, with card_id as the final tie-break.
Handoff boundary: review remediation generates cited ordered directives, an apply-ready prompt, and PENDING log entries; downstream execution and confirmation are outside the Brain.

modes.name: Ask
modes.detail: returns the best relevant cards with citation blocks, or refuses when no card clears the floor

modes.name: Cite
modes.detail: returns one authoritative decision, rule, or definition as a paste-ready footnote; open items are excluded

modes.name: Prep
modes.detail: groups relevant authoritative positions separately from open items for meeting preparation

modes.name: Remediate
modes.detail: generates the cited apply-ready prompt and PENDING change-log handoff from recorded review directives

modes.name: Integrity loop
modes.detail: compares stored cards and directives with the transcript of record, re-derives supported drift, and quarantines orphaned citations

limits.statement: The public runnable corpus is hand-authored and fully fictional; it demonstrates the ingest-and-query stage but does not record or transcribe a live meeting.
limits.source: knowledge-brain-engine/README.md — public runnable version over invented transcripts; brain_engine/generate.py module documentation

limits.statement: Retrieval is deterministic keyword/tag TF-IDF, not semantic embeddings or a language model; a query still needs terms that score against the indexed cards.
limits.source: brain_engine/engine.py — stdlib TF-IDF index, tokenization, weighted tags, and MIN_RELEVANCE

limits.statement: Open items can appear in meeting prep but cannot support cite mode; if no authoritative card clears the floor, the engine refuses.
limits.source: brain_engine/model.py AUTHORITATIVE_KINDS; brain_engine/engine.py cite and prep

limits.statement: The remediation output stops at a cited apply-ready prompt and a change log whose entries begin PENDING; a downstream AI or operator performs the workpaper edits and confirms status.
limits.source: knowledge-brain-engine/README.md Review → remediation; brain_engine/engine.py Remediation and STATUS_PENDING; brain_engine/report.py

limits.statement: The integrity loop can re-derive stored knowledge text or speaker only when the cited transcript utterance exists. Orphaned citations are quarantined rather than repaired from a guess.
limits.source: brain_engine/loop.py — REMEDIABLE excludes ORPHANED; integrity_loop and verdict_exit_code

media.poster_alt: Knowledge Brain system illustration showing meeting and SOP documents entering a queryable knowledge store, with a cited verbatim timestamped answer and a no-source no-answer control
media.caption: Repository system animation: meeting and SOP sources feed the Knowledge Brain; answers leave cited verbatim and timestamped, while an unsourced query returns no answer.

quickstart.label: Clone and print the fictional brain index
Retrieve one paste-ready cited footnote
quickstart.command: python -m brain_engine --cite "return of capital in excess of basis is a deemed gain"

quickstart.label: Generate the cited review-remediation handoff
quickstart.command: python -m brain_engine remediate "Surplus Workpaper Review — Reviewer Corrections"

meta.description: Knowledge Brain (SFS-E07-KBN) datasheet — deterministic, citation-governed retrieval over fictional meeting transcripts, with cited answers, paste-ready footnotes, meeting prep, explicit refusal, and a generated apply-ready remediation handoff whose execution and confirmation stay downstream.
